features.summary: RTX delivered strong Q4 2024 results with adjusted sales of $80.8 billion (up 11% organically), adjusted EPS of $5.73 (up 13%), and free cash flow of $4.5 billion. The company ended with a record $218 billion backlog, including $125 billion commercial and $93 billion defense. All three business segments achieved 90+ basis points of margin expansion. For 2025, RTX expects sales of $83-84 billion (4-6% organic growth), adjusted EPS of $6.00-6.15, and free cash flow of $7.0-7.5 billion.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed confidence in sustained growth driven by robust commercial aerospace demand (IATA estimates 8% RPK growth) and strong defense requirements globally. They expect commercial aftermarket growth of ~10%, mid-single-digit growth in both commercial OE and defense sales. The company anticipates continued segment margin expansion across all three businesses with 10-13% segment profit growth. Management highlighted significant capacity investments, AI implementation across 40+ use cases, and over $7.5 billion planned R&D spending to support future growth.
features.risks: The primary risk remains the GTF powder metal issue, with expected cash outflows of $1.1-1.3 billion in 2025 and residual amounts in 2026. Supply chain constraints continue across structural castings, isothermal forgings, microelectronics, and rocket motors, though some improvements noted. Boeing 737 MAX production ramp uncertainty and potential airline inventory destocking present commercial aerospace risks. Geopolitical tensions and defense program timing changes could impact Raytheon's performance.
features.key_drivers: Commercial aftermarket growth is the primary driver, supported by aging fleet demographics and robust air travel demand. GTF aftermarket margins approaching double-digits with profitable operations for several years. Defense backlog growth driven by international demand (44% of Raytheon backlog now international) and replenishment needs. Productivity improvements through core operating system implementation, with 11% sales growth achieved with only 2% headcount increase. Working capital improvements expected to contribute $1.3 billion cash flow benefit in 2025.
